refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context inputs, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration history.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) each requirement is tagged with source mission context; (c) baseline snapshots are recorded with timestamp and operator identity; (d) all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (e) historical baselines are retrievable and comparable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from mission context using validated algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and tag each derived requirement with source mission context and traceability metadata.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a versioned baseline of performance requirements with timestamp, operator, and approval status.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Log all requirement updates (changes, calibration adjustments) with reason, timestamp, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.6: Provide retrieval and comparison of historical baselines for trend analysis and compliance verification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Requirement Engine (PRE) module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement derivation algorithms, records baselines in a versioned database, and exposes requirement data via REST/message interfaces.
DP-B: Distributed requirement derivation where each vehicle computes its own performance targets locally and syncs to a central baseline registry with eventual consistency.
DP-C: Manual requirement entry with optional algorithmic suggestions; operators review and approve before baseline recording.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: PRE computes targets, but Safety Services validates and approves before baseline lock; all changes flow through a change control workflow.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Centralized Performance Requirement Engine (PRE) with Safety Services validation gate. The PRE ingests mission context, executes requirement derivation algorithms, and proposes baselines. Safety Services reviews and approves all baselines and updates before they are locked in the versioned baseline registry. All changes (requirement updates, calibration adjustments) flow through a formal change control workflow with audit logging. This design ensures: (1) automated derivation efficiency, (2) safety-critical validation (high safety criticality), (3) full traceability and audit trail, (4) compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.

potential_sources: PRE algorithm design is decoupled from Safety Services validation logic; they interact via well-defined approval interface.
Mission context ingestion is decoupled from requirement computation; both are internal to PRE.
Baseline versioning and audit logging are orthogonal to derivation; can be implemented as separate concerns within PRE.
No circular dependencies detected between PRE and Safety Services.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D exhibits low coupling: PRE and Safety Services are loosely coupled via an approval gate interface. The design is modular and allows independent evolution of derivation algorithms and validation policies.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Mission context inputs are well-defined and available at system startup and during operation.
ASSUMPTION: Requirement derivation algorithms are validated and produce deterministic, repeatable results.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services has authority and capability to review and approve performance baselines within acceptable time windows.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be invalid; mitigation: implement validation rules and user feedback loops.
RISK: If derivation algorithms are not validated, baselines may be incorrect; mitigation: require algorithm V&V and sensitivity analysis.
RISK: If Safety Services approval becomes a bottleneck, system responsiveness may degrade; mitigation: define SLAs for approval and escalation paths.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the interface contract between PRE and Safety Services: define what constitutes a 'valid' baseline proposal, what Safety Services must check, and what approval criteria apply. Define SLAs for approval turnaround. Specify fallback behavior if Safety Services is unavailable (e.g., use last approved baseline).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It contains the Performance Requirement Engine (PRE) as a logical subsystem, integrates with Safety Services for validation, and exposes performance requirement data and baselines to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Telemetry and Tasking interfaces. The NGHE Solution owns the requirement derivation logic, baseline versioning, and audit trail.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution publishes current performance baselines, requirement targets, and historical baseline metadata to Vehicle and Teleoperation via Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives mission context inputs (worksite parameters, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager and Operator via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exchanges baseline approval requests and safety validation feedback with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Safety Services and to distribute baselines to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit trail. The PRE logic, versioning, and audit logging are core system functions. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces to Safety Services (validation) and Vehicle/Teleoperation (baseline distribution).

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and reporting telemetry, not for deriving system-level performance requirements. Placing PRE in Vehicle would fragment requirement management across multiple vehicle instances and break traceability. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and monitoring, not for requirement derivation. Placing PRE in Teleoperation would couple requirement management to tele-operation infrastructure and create inconsistency if multiple vehicles are involved. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and alerts, not for requirement derivation. While Safety Services validates baselines, the derivation engine and baseline registry should remain in NGHE Solution to maintain clear separation of concerns. Placing PRE in Safety Services would conflate requirement derivation with safety validation logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Requirement Engine (PRE), baseline versioning, and audit trail are core system-level functions that belong in the NGHE Solution module. Safety Services is a consumer and validator of baselines, not the owner. Vehicle and Teleoperation are consumers of baseline data, not owners. This containment preserves single-source-of-truth for performance requirements and ensures traceability across the entire system.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes too large, PRE may be difficult to test and maintain in isolation. MITIGATION: Design PRE as a well-encapsulated subsystem with clear internal interfaces.
RISK: If Safety Services approval is slow or unavailable, NGHE Solution may be blocked from updating baselines. MITIGATION: Define fallback behavior and SLAs for approval.
RISK: If mission context inputs are not standardized, PRE may receive inconsistent data. MITIGATION: Define a schema for mission context and validate inputs at the Tasking interface.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the PRE subsystem boundary within NGHE Solution, including its internal components (mission context parser, requirement derivation engine, baseline versioning store, audit logger). Define the interface contract with Safety Services (baseline proposal format, approval criteria, response SLA). Specify the Telemetry and Tasking interface schemas for performance requirement data.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (Hybrid PRE with Safety Services validation) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) automatic derivation from mission context (PRE ingestion and computation), (2) baseline recording with traceability (versioned baseline registry), (3) managed history with audit trail (change control workflow and audit logging). The design is traceable to acceptance criteria and incorporates high safety criticality via Safety Services validation gate.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If PRE is not properly encapsulated within NGHE Solution, requirement derivation logic may leak into Vehicle or Teleoperation modules. MITIGATION: Define clear module boundaries and enforce via architecture review.

DIFFUSION RISK: If baseline data is replicated across multiple modules without a single source of truth, consistency and traceability will degrade. MITIGATION: Ensure NGHE Solution is the authoritative baseline registry; other modules consume via Telemetry interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for PRE and baseline management. The module has sufficient scope and authority to own requirement derivation, versioning, and audit trail. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and support the required interactions with Safety Services, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Site Manager.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Requirement derivation can be tested via unit tests of PRE algorithms with known mission context inputs. (2) Baseline recording and traceability can be verified via integration tests that confirm baselines are tagged with source mission context. (3) Audit trail can be verified via log inspection and historical baseline retrieval tests. (4) Safety Services validation can be verified via approval workflow tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns CR-NGHE-005 and its sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.6). Safety Services owns the validation and approval of baselines (sub-FR CR-NGHE-005.4). Vehicle and Teleoperation own the consumption and use of baseline data (implicit in their operational FRs). Site Manager and Operator own the provision of mission context inputs (implicit in their operational roles).

recommended_revisions: Refine the interface contract between NGHE Solution and Safety Services: define baseline proposal schema, approval criteria, and response SLA.
Define the mission context input schema for the Tasking interface: specify required fields (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) and validation rules.
Specify the baseline data schema for the Telemetry interface: include requirement targets, baseline version, timestamp, and traceability metadata.
Define fallback behavior if Safety Services is unavailable: e.g., use last approved baseline or alert operator.
Clarify ownership of audit logging: is it owned by NGHE Solution or by a shared logging service?

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been refined to atomic sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.6) that are ready for further decomposition. The selected DP (DP-D: Hybrid PRE with Safety Services validation) is sound and well-coupled. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate and has high containment score (95). All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The next level of decomposition should address each sub-FR individually, starting with CR-NGHE-005.1 (mission context ingestion) and CR-NGHE-005.2 (requirement computation). This will allow detailed design of the PRE subsystem and its internal components.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from mission context using validated algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and tag each derived requirement with source mission context and traceability metadata.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a versioned baseline of performance requirements with timestamp, operator, and approval status.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Log all requirement updates (changes, calibration adjustments) with reason, timestamp, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.6: Provide retrieval and comparison of historical baselines for trend analysis and compliance verification.

assumptions: Mission context inputs are well-defined and available at system startup and during operation.
Requirement derivation algorithms are validated and produce deterministic, repeatable results.
Safety Services has authority and capability to review and approve performance baselines within acceptable time windows.
NGHE Solution has sufficient computational and storage capacity to run PRE and maintain baseline history.
Network Link is reliable and has sufficient bandwidth to distribute baselines to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be invalid.
If derivation algorithms are not validated, baselines may be incorrect.
If Safety Services approval becomes a bottleneck, system responsiveness may degrade.
If baseline data is replicated across modules without a single source of truth, consistency and traceability will degrade.
If PRE is not properly encapsulated, requirement derivation logic may leak into other modules.
evidence_needed: Validated requirement derivation algorithms with sensitivity analysis and error bounds.
Mission context input schema and validation rules.
Baseline data schema and interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).
Safety Services approval workflow and SLA definition.
Audit logging design and retention policy.
Fallback behavior specification for Safety Services unavailability.
Integration test plan for PRE, Safety Services, Vehicle, and Teleoperation interactions.
